# UC-18.3.20 · Fabric Host Route and ARP Scale Headroom

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity, Availability, Inventory &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch how full each switch's hidden forwarding shelves are—the spots where routes and neighbor entries live. When those shelves overflow, traffic can vanish quietly even though cables blink green, so we sound the alarm before people feel random slowdowns.*

---

## Description

EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP host routes and ARP or IPv6 neighbor growth consume route-table-v4, route-table-v6, MAC-table, ARP, IPv6-ND, and multicast-route hardware classes on merchant leaf switches. Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS hardware capacity profiles—vxlan-bgp, default, or lan-host-routes—and Nexus 9504 or 9508 chassis pooling across line cards set how fast shared banks fill. Arista EOS system mode profiles (default-routes, L2, L3-ECMP, unicast-routing) remap TCAM via FAP counters. Juniper Junos forwarding-options enhanced-hash-key and system services hardware-table tuning resize QFX partitions. SONiC lpm-route-stats and REST route-summary plus syncd ASIC counters expose occupancy. Near-capacity operation causes eviction, FIB-realized versus RIB-installed divergence, and silent black-hole risk with Established BGP. Splunk ties NX-API proc-fwd-mgr telemetry, Arista TCAM usage, Junos PFE stats, SONiC summaries, gNMI openconfig-platform components, NDFC inventory, and Nexus Dashboard Insights site_health headroom widgets to onboarding timing.

## Value

When forwarding shelves fill, traffic can drop intermittently without clean BGP faults because silicon evicts entries before operators notice EVPN churn. Tracking per-class utilization, eviction bursts, Type-2 counts versus ceilings, ARP aging noise, and FIB-RIB deltas buys time to retune profiles, summarize routes, scale line cards, or shift tenants before silent black holes hit production.

## Implementation

Normalize FIELDALIAS chains for **device_name**, **hardware_table_class**, and numeric counters across TA parsers; schedule five-minute unions over **`cisco:nxos:tcam`** + **`arista:eos:tcam`** before layering **NDI** headroom widgets. **Quick win:** enable scripted **`show hardware capacity forwarding`** plus **`show platform fap counters tcam usage`** polls into those sourcetypes first—dashboards populate within one business day.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites** — Pin Splunkbase releases 6876 (Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus), 4291 (Splunk_TA_arista), 2847 (**Splunk_TA_junos**) on Heavy Forwarders sized for HTTPS bursts toward **NX-API**, **eAPI**, **NETCONF**, and **SONiC REST** endpoints; pair **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (1546) or hardened Python modular inputs where TLS pinning exceeds stock limits; deploy **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (7777) so bundled tiles reuse canonical sourcetypes without **FIELDALIAS** forks; optionally provision Splunk ITSI Predictive Analytics macros referencing Performance KPI templates when finance reviewers demand thirty-day horizons; allocate least-privilege **network_observer** roles with **srchIndexesAllowed** spanning **dc_fabric**, **network_ops**, **cisco_dc**, os, **sdn_ops** per governance naming; rotate OAuth secrets via Splunk credential stores rather than plaintext paste beside collectors; reserve indexer retention tiers covering ninety-day investigative windows plus yearly archives for procurement audits referencing chassis upgrades; document poll interval guardrails so NX-API storms never starve ISSU orchestration bridges scheduled elsewhere.

**Step 1 — Data sources** — Cisco Nexus 9000: poll NX-API REST **GET /api/mo/sys/proc/proc-fwd-mgr/hwcapacity.json** alongside scripted **show hardware capacity forwarding** snapshots (60–300s) stamped **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:tcam`**; annotate **vxlan-bgp**, default, **lan-host-routes** hardware capacity profile identifiers whenever paired **show hardware profile** collectors emit templates differing from legacy defaults; capture **show hardware capacity module** excerpts describing Nexus 9504 or Nexus 9508 multi-line-card forwarding-table sharing boundaries so Splunk preserves control-plane versus dataplane attribution across slots; supplement **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:syslog`** for MNEMONIC families HWRESOURCE-*, ARP-*, L2RIB-* narratives revealing eviction wording absent structured counters; hydrate **GET https://<nd-cluster>/sedgeapi/v1/site_health** into **`cisco:ndi:health`** JSON focusing per-switch **table-headroom** widgets; enrich **`cisco:ndfc:fabric`** inventory with **fabric_token**, chassis serial, linecard slots. Arista EOS: drive **`arista:eos:tcam`** using **eAPI** bodies issuing **show platform fap counters tcam usage** plus **show platform fap counters fec usage** JSON aligned to poll cadences; stamp **system mode** tuples default-routes, L2, **L3-ECMP**, unicast-routing extracted from **show system mode** companion polls because TCAM carve-outs pivot with mode migrations. Juniper Junos: populate **`juniper:junos:pfe`** via NETCONF **get-pfe-statistics** RPC plus **show pfe statistics tcam-summary** transcripts; correlate **forwarding-options enhanced-hash-key** settings and **system services hardware-table** tuning knobs referencing **show chassis hardware** for **QFX5120** forwarding complexes. SONiC: ingest **`sonic:show:route_summary`** using REST **GET /api/v1/sonic/show/route-summary** augmented by redis-cli COUNTERS_DB pulls surfacing **lpm-route-stats** while **syncd** publishes vendor ASIC SDK counters mirrored into Splunk via **HEC** batches sized for five-minute cadences without starving collectors. gNMI: subscribe **openconfig-platform** components paths delivering **linecard_id**, removable, empty hints pairing Splunk rows with physical slots during Nexus 9508 investigations. **Quick win:** ship **`cisco:nxos:tcam`** plus **`arista:eos:tcam`** feeds first—Splunk heatmaps illuminate headroom slopes before **NDI** widgets finish OAuth onboarding.

**Step 2 — Field extractions** — Encode **props.conf** and **transforms.conf** extracting **device_name**, vendor, linecard_id, **hardware_table_class** enumerations **route-table-v4**, **route-table-v6**, **MAC-table**, ARP, **IPv6-ND**, **multicast-route**, **entries_used**, **entries_max**, **headroom_percent** macro-computed when absent at ingest, **eviction_count_5min**, **evpn_type2_route_count**, **arp_table_size**, **fib_rib_divergence_count**, **last_table_overflow_ts**, **node_role** leaf versus spine; maintain **vendor_table_label** aside canonical classes for Arista versus Cisco terminology alignment; map **fabric_token** lookups to **NDFC** fabric names; stamp **capacity_profile** whenever hardware capacity profile strings appear inside **_raw** text; **FIELDALIAS** **evpn_rt2_host**, **type2_routes**, **rt2_count** into **evpn_type2_route_count** so EVPN analytics stay multi-vendor; dedupe overlapping modular inputs landing duplicate poll cycles following HF restarts documented inside ops logs.

**Step 3 — Initial validation** — Compare Splunk latest() telemetry against CLI references **show hardware capacity forwarding**, show platform fap counters tcam usage, show pfe statistics tcam-summary, **show ip route summary**, **show lpmroutes summary** capturing golden screenshots archived beside validation tickets; reconcile NDI table-headroom percentages with **cisco:ndi:health** payloads inside one polling interval; contrast Arista CloudVision Hardware Insights and Juniper Apstra capacity graphs solely as directional parity checks—Splunk macros remain authoritative when parsers disagree slightly; audit linecard_id coverage on Nexus 9504 and Nexus 9508 chassis comparing **show module** outputs so pooled forwarding budgets never collapse unrelated slots behind duplicated hostnames; investigate EPS bursts whenever collectors flatten concurrent polls across thousands of leaf switches sharing one Heavy Forwarder CPU peg.

**Step 4 — SPL building blocks** — Maintain five-minute bins recomputing headroom_percent per hardware_table_class and device_name; treat eviction_count_5min greater than zero as silent black-hole risk independent of syslog severity because silicon eviction precedes control-plane alarms; chart **type2_vs_capacity_ratio** highlighting **EVPN Type-2** host-route counts versus allocated hardware route-table ceilings before BGP exposes faults; correlate arp_table_size deltas with ARP aging timers parsed from **show ip arp timeout** streams isolating churn storms from intentional timer edits; escalate **fib_rib_divergence_count** paired with **eviction_anomaly** because **FIB-realized** lag versus **RIB-installed** installs signals dataplane divergence; wrap **streamstats** slopes feeding **forecast_30d_headroom** exhaustion narratives consumed by procurement reviewers; encapsulate macros **fabric_tc_headroom_base** referencing governance-approved index unions so investigators inherit identical SPL scaffolding across tenants; throttle alerts per fabric_token so overlapping storms map to singular CAB bridges rather than duplicated paging floods.

**Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns** — Merge NDI site_health posture widgets with Splunk timelines revealing aggregated anomalies invisible inside siloed vendor dashboards alone; JOIN CMDB rack_position, cabinet_row, power_zone lookups whenever linecard_id clustering concentrates faults physically inside Nexus 9508 inserts requiring hardware logistics—not mere ACL tweaks; correlate ServiceNow **CHG** short descriptions referencing tenant onboarding, **VRF** expansions, **L3Out** announcements explaining spikes in route-table-v4 or evpn_type2_route_count counters; audit hardware capacity profile edits on **NX-OS** requiring ISSU using change metadata because **entries_max** shifts without CAB breadcrumbs deserve escalation; tie Arista **TCAM profile** reload maintenance windows documented under EOS advisories to **arista:eos:tcam** slope inflections; layer Splunk ITSI Predictive Analytics or predict SPL atop headroom_percent series powering thirty-day exhaustion forecasts bundled into quarterly capacity decks; annotate MITRE ATTACK **T1499.001** overlays where deliberate redistribution storms mimic denial-of-service via resource exhaustion distinct from organic tenant scale; attribute tenants via **vrf_name**, tenant_tag, bridge_domain extracted from NDFC or Apstra exports isolating noisy workloads; archive pre-upgrade hardware-table-headroom evidence packs capturing CLI transcripts listed earlier plus Splunk screenshots stored inside evidence_index with immutable timestamps satisfying audit sampling; integrate SOAR playbooks referencing change_request_id bridges so remediation reopens only when soak timers expire without renewed eviction positives.

**Step 6 — Operational runbook** — Declare **Sev-2** when headroom_percent stays below fifteen percent across three successive five-minute buckets on production leaf switches; escalate **Sev-1** when eviction_count_5min remains positive or fib_rib_divergence_count climbs concurrently with eviction anomalies implying active silent black-hole forwarding risk despite Established BGP peers; cite vendor MTBF guidance for hardware-table stability alongside operational MTTR medians mined from ServiceNow closures segmented by hardware_table_class; publish Dashboard Studio tokens fabric_token, device_name, hardware_table_class aligning heatmaps, eviction timelines, Type-2 trends, fib_rib_divergence_count panels, predictive **forecast_30d_headroom** tiles described in visualization; route first response to NetOps fabric squads, second to ASIC platform engineering only when eviction storms reproduce across vendors, third to workload owners when tenant attribution overlays dominate Splunk facets; weekly governance reconciles Splunk exhaustion forecasts with finance triggers for line-card purchases or route summarization programs; rotate API credentials quarterly recording vault ticket bridges for auditor traceability; maintain war-room etiquette documenting MTTR start at alert acknowledgement and MTTR stop at validated headroom_percent recovery verified via CLI snapshots archived inside incidents.

**Governance annex** — Maintain architecture decision records stating why Splunk-normalized TCAM telemetry beats spreadsheet screenshots for auditors; archive saved-search revisions weekly whenever FIELDALIAS transforms shift during TA upgrades so reviewers correlate alert firing changes with parser cadence rather than unexplained drift; extend lookup retention for fabric_token_to_site.csv inside Git-reviewed repositories mirroring CAB approvals; document credential custodians beside Splunk secret stores—never embed operator LDAP passwords inside modular inputs merely because expedient testing demanded shortcuts during outage bridges.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:tcam" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:tcam" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:pfe" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:route_summary")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval device_name=trim(coalesce(device_name, hostname, switch_name, host, ""))
| eval vendor=lower(trim(coalesce(vendor, manufacturer, vendor_name, "")))
| eval linecard_id=trim(coalesce(linecard_id, module_id, slot_id, lc_slot, ""))
| eval node_role=lower(trim(coalesce(node_role, switch_role, fabric_role, device_role, "leaf")))
| eval hardware_table_class=lower(trim(coalesce(hardware_table_class, table_class, resource_class, hw_resource, "")))
| eval entries_used=tonumber(coalesce(entries_used, used_entries, current_entries, occupied_entries, ""))
| eval entries_max=tonumber(coalesce(entries_max, max_entries, limit_entries, capacity_entries, table_limit, ""))
| eval eviction_count_5min=tonumber(coalesce(eviction_count_5min, hw_eviction_events, eviction_events, evict_cnt, ""))
| eval evpn_type2_route_count=tonumber(coalesce(evpn_type2_route_count, evpn_rt2_host, rt2_count, type2_routes, ""))
| eval arp_table_size=tonumber(coalesce(arp_table_size, arp_entries, neighbor_v4_count, ""))
| eval fib_rib_divergence_count=tonumber(coalesce(fib_rib_divergence_count, fib_rib_mismatch, fib_rib_delta, ""))
| eval headroom_percent=if(isnotnull(entries_max) AND entries_max>0 AND isnotnull(entries_used), round(100*(entries_max-entries_used)/entries_max, 2), null())
| eval type2_vs_capacity_ratio=if(isnotnull(entries_max) AND entries_max>0 AND isnotnull(evpn_type2_route_count), round(100*evpn_type2_route_count/entries_max, 2), null())
| eval eviction_anomaly=if(isnotnull(eviction_count_5min) AND eviction_count_5min>0, 1, 0)
| eval headroom_sev=case(isnotnull(headroom_percent) AND headroom_percent<15, "sev2_sustained_low", isnotnull(headroom_percent) AND headroom_percent<25, "sev3_watch", 1=1, "nominal")
| eval blackhole_risk=if(eviction_anomaly=1 OR (isnotnull(headroom_percent) AND headroom_percent<10), "elevated", "normal")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(entries_used) AS entries_used latest(entries_max) AS entries_max latest(headroom_percent) AS headroom_percent latest(eviction_count_5min) AS eviction_count_5min latest(evpn_type2_route_count) AS evpn_type2_route_count latest(type2_vs_capacity_ratio) AS type2_vs_capacity_ratio latest(arp_table_size) AS arp_table_size latest(fib_rib_divergence_count) AS fib_rib_divergence_count latest(eviction_anomaly) AS eviction_anomaly latest(headroom_sev) AS headroom_sev latest(blackhole_risk) AS blackhole_risk BY _time device_name vendor hardware_table_class linecard_id node_role
| streamstats window=6 global=f latest(headroom_percent) AS headroom_prev BY device_name hardware_table_class
| eval headroom_slope=if(isnotnull(headroom_prev), headroom_percent-headroom_prev, 0)
| eval forecast_30d_headroom=if(isnotnull(headroom_slope), round(headroom_percent+(headroom_slope*8640), 2), null())
| where headroom_percent<20 OR eviction_anomaly=1 OR type2_vs_capacity_ratio>85 OR fib_rib_divergence_count>0 OR headroom_sev!="nominal"
| sort - eviction_anomaly - fib_rib_divergence_count headroom_percent
| head 400
| table _time device_name vendor linecard_id node_role hardware_table_class entries_used entries_max headroom_percent eviction_count_5min evpn_type2_route_count type2_vs_capacity_ratio arp_table_size fib_rib_divergence_count forecast_30d_headroom headroom_sev blackhole_risk
```

## Visualization

(1) **Heatmap** — **`hardware_table_class`** × **`device_name`** colored by **`headroom_percent`** (green >25%, amber 15–25%, red <15%). (2) **Timechart** — **`eviction_count_5min`** stacked by **`vendor`** with **`eviction_anomaly`** overlays. (3) **Area chart** — **`evpn_type2_route_count`** versus **`entries_max`** lines per **`device_name`**. (4) **Column chart** — **`fib_rib_divergence_count`** stacked by **`fabric_token`** after lookup enrichment. (5) **Single value** + **sparkline** — **`forecast_30d_headroom`** worst case per class with trend. (6) Optional **drilldown table** — **`arp_table_size`** deltas correlated with **`node_role`**.

## Known False Positives

**ISSU hardware-capacity-profile reload windows** — engineered ISSU sequences temporarily bounce **vxlan-bgp**/**lan-host-routes** profiles on **Nexus 9000**, producing abrupt **entries_max** shifts and transient **headroom_percent** dips without sustained **eviction_count_5min** positives—suppress via **CHG** tokens referencing ISSU orchestration plus **MaintenanceWindow** lookups keyed on **device_name** until soak timers expire.

**External Type-5 prefix surges at L3Out hand-offs** — DC perimeter routers announce large aggregated prefixes during coordinated **L3Out** migrations, spiking **route-table-v4** occupancy on border leaves even though **evpn_type2_route_count** remains flat—differentiate using **hardware_table_class** route-table-v4 focus, **BGP** **AS-PATH** correlation, and **NDFC** change tickets before attributing spikes to faulty host-route scaling alone.

**VRF-stretch ARP reconciliation sweeps** — after **VRF** stretch or **L2VNI** replay events, NetOps intentionally clear ARP caches or shorten aging timers causing **arp_table_size** oscillation and benign **eviction_count_5min** blips tied to **ARP** class—pair Splunk timelines with **ServiceNow** **CHG** narratives describing cache refresh rather than hardware faults.

**BGP soft-reconfig FIB staging** — during **soft-reconfiguration inbound** maintenance, **FIB-realized** entries can lag **RIB-installed** counts for minutes while **fib_rib_divergence_count** increments—expected until **route-refresh** completes; suppress when **BGP** **soft-reconfig** flags appear in **cisco:nxos:syslog** and **peer** sessions stay **Established**.

**PIM RP cutovers** — multicast-route hardware occupancy spikes during rendezvous-point migrations unrelated to EVPN Type-2 counts—cross-check multicast CRQ tickets before escalating TCAM defects.
